title: Delicate Smoky Japanese Cabbage Chawanmushi
description: A light and festive Japanese dish featuring cabbage and smoke, made in a traditional chawanmushi style, with a resinous flavor profile that is dash-friendly.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
Prepare the dashi broth according to the package instructions or recipe.
In a blender or food processor, puree the chopped cabbage and dashi broth until smooth.
Strain the mixture through a fine-mesh sieve into a bowl, discarding the solids.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the mirin, soy sauce, and eggs.
Add the smoked salmon, sesame oil, salt, and white pepper to the egg mixture and stir to combine.
Add the cabbage and dashi mixture to the egg mixture and stir gently to combine.
Pour the mixture into small cups or ramekins and place them in a large baking dish.
Add hot water to the baking dish to come halfway up the sides of the cups.
Bake for 25 minutes, or until the edges are set and the centers are still slightly jiggly.
